mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 4.0% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal triggers in 80%+ of cases without corresponding returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risks materialize.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where both GDPC1 < 2.0% and HOUST < 1.0% simultaneously, or the combined signal's Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in CPI inflation above 3.0% triggers central bank hawkishness, causing bond yields to rise and equity valuations to contract, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 3.0% or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.0 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO > 105.0 or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates deteriorating demand conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where both RSAFS < 470,000 and UNRATE > 4.5% or the interaction signal's Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 signals strong labor market performance, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through elevated consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 150,000 or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.0 indicates weakening supply chain activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO < 104.0 or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03.
